This graph about, you know, 45 percent of communication is body language 45 percent of communication is tonality and 10 percent is the words and when I first heard that it was really Banner and NLP And I was like, I don't bull that does not make sense to me whatsoever Just doesn't how could only 10% of my communication be words when if I put myself in the other person's shoes Like I'm being sold to when a salesman is selling me. I am hanging on every word I'm analyzing the meaning of each word what the phrase means you get I am like I'm hanging on their words So to say that 10% of communication is words just is on its face. Just it's a prime facial nonsense, right? And the interesting thing is that that is out there like in the whole world of Communication training and NLP, you know, people just take that at face value and the problem with it is that's not what bears Banner had meant Unfortunately, the people who teach NLP don't go into that at all. So when I started actually taking it through and when I really said I First learned NLP before I created even straight line 1.0 really 2.0 the front of the box system I many years back I created I actually went through NLP training so I want to really understand my own strategy and I was fortunate that Banner read my book We became good friends, right? but the the point was is that I Had it really thought it through when I first heard of my 45 on hmm that strikes me as being just nonsensical Right, but then when I actually start to think it's just oh wait a second. I see what's going on what what it means really is that 90% of the time We are communicating without speaking You get it like in other words. There's so much unconscious communication that if you took in all Communication you find that only 10% of the time the communication was a result of words The rest of the time you're communicating through your body language and your tonality Grunts groans how you dress how you your facial expressions, right? You're even a little laughter how you laugh all these things. That's what's going on. However in those 10% of the times when you Open up your mouth and actually speak Your words matter 100% You get it the words have to be right The words matter they matter more than anything, you know what because I'll tell you this because if the words Don't add up You can't a create an airtight logical case. This is how I feel It's not just that you don't just use tonality as a salesperson to communicate how you feel So someone understands that you're sharp on the ball and expert your enthusiastic, right? That's the outgoing communication, but the incoming communication from the prospect They're full well understanding what they're layering on to you with their unconscious communication They are telling you how they feel when someone responds who says that sounds pretty good They're telling you, you know, sorry buddy. I'm really not liking what you're saying that much. I'm just being polite What do you know? It sounds really good They're saying oh wow, I'm pretty close here if you tell me more make me really certain and make me comfortable across my action threshold I'll probably buy right now if I could afford it They're telling you that and they want you to know that that's why they apply the tonality We apply if not, we'd be like, hmm sound I'd be reserved people say oh make sure you continue So don't let them know how excited you are. So yet in those we've been taught Don't tip your hand make sure you keep on your poker face What is that about your poker face your poker face is about body language and tonality tipping the hand Unconsciously of what you're really feeling inside It's a great idea for that's a good new analogy for the day. I'm gonna put a graphic of people playing poker, right? There's a good. It's a perfect people actually have tells Will they actually reveal whether they're being honest or not? With body language and tonality they have tells The point I'm making here and I think what a lot of people miss with the straight line is that this Communication with tonality and body language it happens both ways You don't need it's not enough for you to master the art of applying Body language and tonality at the highest level that's you must do that. You also must master reading it Understanding someone's accused the social cues that allow you to move through the world and be elegant You can't do that without having a really deep Understanding of what is actually going on. I know it's true, but Is it really that I'm telling you if you don't get these first four seconds? Right there is nothing that moves past it. I Am not saying that if you do the first few seconds, right, you'll close the deal. It's not what I'm saying I'm saying as you saw in this module. I use the analogy many times. I call death by a thousand cuts Right, so cut cut you keep making a little mistake here a little mistake there little error here with body language How you perceive before you know your 12 seconds in and you bled out They've tuned out. That's what happens when you bleed out. They tune out Too many mistakes you revealed yourself as being a novice not an expert That's really not interested in what you're doing. You're not enthusiastic. You're not excited about it And you're not a person that's gonna solve their problems and what that chunges up to is that chunk of chunk up? You're not a person worth listening to you're snot Sorry, I'm not gonna take time out of my busy day to listen. I'm not gonna see control I'm not gonna hand you control. It's really really hot and then you habituate and it feels fine. That's how the brain works You have to modulate and constantly speeding up slowing down changing to now.
